drugs.notes: Dose banded at 10mg/ml. Calvert formula using calculated GFR. AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Max dose 890mg (set in ARIA to match national dose bands; internationally recommended max 900mg). Dose may need adjusting if renal function changes by more than 10% between cycles. Seek consultant advice for obese patients or CrCl >125ml/min.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1x10^9/L — counts remain low after 7-day delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction. If myelosuppression recurs after reduction, stop treatment.
Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets 50-99x10^9/L — counts remain in range after delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction.
Reduce dose by 50%.
No dose adjustment necessary.
Do not use. GFR changes >10% between cycles may require dose adjustment.

pre_medications: Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Allopurinol (for those at risk of tumour lysis syndrome — begin day before chemotherapy, continue while significant chemosensitive tumour bulk remains)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2 of cycle)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take home)
special_warnings: SCLC can be very sensitive to chemotherapy — risk of tumour lysis syndrome at start of therapy; prescribe allopurinol for at-risk patients.
Second line treatment of SCLC — WHO Performance Status 0 or 1 required.
Carboplatin is an irritant — take precautions to avoid extravasation.
Consider blood transfusion or erythropoietin (per NICE TA323) if haemoglobin <8g/dL or patient symptomatic of anaemia.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated in subsequent cycles without consultant approval.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required.

drugs.notes: Dose banded at 10mg/ml. Calvert formula using calculated GFR. AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Max dose 890mg (ARIA; internationally 900mg). Dose may need adjusting if renal function changes >10% between cycles. Seek consultant advice for obese patients or CrCl >125ml/min.
Administered as IV bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose band (10mg/ml). Maximum dose 60mg. Vinorelbine is a vesicant.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1.5x10^9/L — counts remain low after delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction. Stop if myelosuppression recurs.
Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <100x10^9/L — counts remain low after delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction.
Reduce dose by 50%.
No dose reduction necessary.
Consider dose reduction to 20mg/m² in severe liver impairment for intravenous preparation.
Do not administer if CrCl <20ml/min. GFR changes >10% may require dose adjustment.
No dose adjustment necessary.
Dose adjustments for day 8 should be made according to local practice guidelines or procedures.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 only
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 only
Metoclopramide 10mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 8 only
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2 of cycle)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take home)
special_warnings: First line therapy of stage III or IV NSCLC — palliative intent. WHO Performance Status 0, 1, 2.
Carboplatin is an irritant; Vinorelbine is a vesicant — take appropriate extravasation precautions.
Consider blood transfusion if patient symptomatic of anaemia or haemoglobin <8g/dL.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated in subsequent cycles without consultant approval.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required.

drugs.notes: Dose banded at 10mg/ml. Calvert formula using calculated GFR. AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Max dose 890mg (ARIA; internationally 900mg). Dose may need adjusting if renal function changes >10% between cycles. Seek consultant advice for obese patients or CrCl >125ml/min.
Administered as IV bolus in 50ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Dose banded per national dose band (10mg/ml). Maximum dose 60mg. Vinorelbine is a vesicant.

Oral capsules (available in 20mg and 30mg strengths). Dose rounded to nearest 20mg (up if halfway). Maximum oral dose 120mg for 60mg/m² dose. Must be swallowed whole with food — not chewed, sucked, or dissolved. Must not be supplied from primary care. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert applies.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1.5x10^9/L — counts remain low after delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction. Stop if myelosuppression recurs.
Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <100x10^9/L — counts remain low after delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction.
Reduce dose by 50%.
No dose reduction necessary.
Consider reduction to 20mg/m² IV in severe liver impairment.
Consider dose of 50mg/m²/week for oral preparation in moderate liver impairment.
Do not administer if CrCl <20ml/min. GFR changes >10% may require dose adjustment.
No dose adjustment necessary.
Dose adjustments for day 8 should be made according to local practice guidelines or procedures.

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 only
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 only
Metoclopramide 10mg oral 15-30 minutes prior to vinorelbine on day 8 only
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take home, starting day 2 of cycle)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take home)
special_warnings: First line therapy of stage III or IV NSCLC — palliative intent. WHO Performance Status 0, 1, 2.
Carboplatin is an irritant; IV Vinorelbine is a vesicant — take appropriate extravasation precautions.
Oral vinorelbine capsules must be swallowed whole with food — do not chew, suck or dissolve.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert must be followed for oral vinorelbine.
Oral vinorelbine must not be supplied from primary care — short term therapy only.
Consider blood transfusion if patient symptomatic of anaemia or haemoglobin <8g/dL.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
All dose reductions should not be re-escalated in subsequent cycles without consultant approval.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required.

drugs.notes: Administered in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes via 0.2 to 5 micron in-line low protein binding filter. Pembrolizumab is administered BEFORE carboplatin and paclitaxel. 4 cycles of combination phase followed by pembrolizumab monotherapy continuation (200mg every 3 weeks for 31 cycles or 400mg every 6 weeks for 16 cycles) for up to 2 years total. Dose reductions NOT permitted for pembrolizumab — interrupt for toxicity. Extravasation classification: neutral.

Administered in 500ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 180 minutes. Must be given via non-PVC administration set with in-line 0.22 micron filter. Dose banded per national dose bands (6mg/ml). Paclitaxel is a vesicant. Hypersensitivity reactions may occur — interrupt infusion for minor symptoms; discontinue immediately for severe reactions (profound hypotension, bronchospasm, generalised erythema).

Administered in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes. Dose banded at 10mg/ml. Calvert formula using calculated GFR. AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Max dose 890mg (ARIA; internationally 900mg). Dose may need adjusting if renal function changes >10% between cycles. Seek consultant advice for obese patients or CrCl >125ml/min. Extravasation: irritant.

dose_modifications.notes: Proceed at 100% dose.
Delay for 7 days. If counts recover to ≥1x10^9/L within this time, continue at full dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1x10^9/L — persistent after delay or repeated delays
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery then reduce dose by 20%.
Proceed at 100% dose.
Delay for 7 days. If counts recover to ≥100x10^9/L within this time, continue at full dose.
Delay until recovery then reduce dose by 20%.
Delay until recovery then reduce dose by 50%.
No dose adjustment needed.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment Bilirubin <21 µmol/L and AST/ALT <10xULN — Paclitaxel
dose_modifications.notes: 100% dose.
75% of original dose.
45% of original dose.
30% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment Bilirubin >85 µmol/L or AST/ALT >10xULN — Paclitaxel
dose_modifications.notes: Contraindicated.

No dose adjustment required for mild hepatic impairment. For hepatitis with AST/ALT 3-5xULN and/or bilirubin 1.5-3xULN: withhold and administer corticosteroids. Permanently discontinue if hepatic injury does not improve to NCI-CTC grade 1 within 12 weeks.
Omit. GFR changes >10% may require dose adjustment.
No dose adjustment needed.

dose_modifications.condition: Renal impairment mild to moderate — Pembrolizumab
dose_modifications.notes: No dose adjustment required. For NCI-CTC grade 2 nephritis: withhold and administer corticosteroids.
Withhold paclitaxel until neuropathy recovers to NCI-CTC grade 1, then reduce to 75% of original dose.
Withhold paclitaxel until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 then reduce to 50%. Discontinue if neuropathy does not resolve to NCI-CTC grade 1.

dose_modifications.condition: Pembrolizumab immune-related adverse reactions — generally interrupt rather than dose reduce
dose_modifications.notes: Pembrolizumab is generally interrupted for immune toxicities rather than dose-reduced. Permanently discontinue for: NCI-CTC grade 3 pneumonitis, nephritis, infusion-related reaction, hepatitis, myocarditis, encephalitis, Guillan-Barre syndrome; any NCI-CTC grade 4 or recurrent grade 3 reaction (except controlled endocrinopathies); recurrent grade 2 nephritis. May restart within 12 weeks if reaction ≤grade 1 and corticosteroid ≤10mg/day prednisolone equivalent.

pre_medications: Pembrolizumab 200mg IV infusion in 100ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 30 minutes (administered first)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV 30 minutes before paclitaxel (hypersensitivity prophylaxis)
Dexamethasone 20mg IV 30 minutes before paclitaxel (hypersensitivity prophylaxis)
H2 antagonist according to local formulary choice and availability 30 minutes before paclitaxel (e.g. ranitidine 50mg IV, famotidine 20mg oral, nizatidine 150mg oral, or ranitidine 150mg oral)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (cycles 1-4 only)
Dexamethasone 4mg oral twice a day for 3 days (take home, starting day 2 of cycle — cycles 1-4 only)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day as required (take home — cycles 1-4 only)
Chlorphenamine 10mg IV when required for infusion-related reactions
Hydrocortisone 100mg IV when required for infusion-related reactions
Paracetamol 1000mg oral when required for infusion-related reactions
special_warnings: Stage IIIB/IIIC or stage IV squamous NSCLC only. PD-L1 TPS testing mandatory before treatment — numeric result must be known.
PD-L1 TPS 0-49% or TPS 50-100% requiring urgent clinical response are eligible. TPS 50-100% patients should have detailed discussion about pembrolizumab monotherapy vs combination.
No prior cytotoxic chemotherapy for advanced/metastatic disease (prior neoadjuvant/adjuvant/concurrent chemo-radiotherapy allowed if completed ≥6 months before recurrence).
No prior anti-PD-1, anti-PD-L1, anti-PD-L2, anti-CD137 or anti-CTLA-4 antibody therapy.
No symptomatic active brain metastases or leptomeningeal metastases.
WHO Performance Status 0 or 1 required. Chemotherapy doses in this regimen are higher than common practice — careful patient selection required.
3-weekly pembrolizumab 200mg must be used during combination phase (not 6-weekly 400mg dosing).
Nab-paclitaxel is NOT commissioned for this indication — only paclitaxel is approved.
Formal medical review of combination therapy continuation scheduled by end of first 6 weeks of treatment.
Treatment breaks up to 12 weeks beyond expected cycle length allowed to allow immune toxicities to settle.
Pembrolizumab dose reductions are NOT permitted — interrupt for immune toxicities.

Pembrolizumab associated with immune-related adverse reactions: pneumonitis, nephritis, colitis, thyroid disorders, hypophysitis, infusion reactions, hepatitis — early diagnosis and management essential.
Paclitaxel must be administered via non-PVC set with in-line 0.22 micron filter.
Paclitaxel is a vesicant; carboplatin is an irritant.
Monitor thyroid function tests at baseline then every 3-6 weeks. Random blood cortisol at baseline then every 3-6 weeks. Random blood glucose prior to each pembrolizumab dose.
Ensure patient has been given a pembrolizumab Patient Alert Card.
After 4 cycles of combination therapy, continue pembrolizumab monotherapy (200mg q3w for 31 cycles or 400mg q6w for 16 cycles) for up to 2 years total treatment duration.
Gastric protection with H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.

drugs.notes: Administered in 100ml glucose 5% or sodium chloride 0.9% over 10 minutes. Choice of fluid depends on product stocked by pharmacy. Pemetrexed is an inflammitant. Carboplatin must be administered 30 minutes after the end of pemetrexed infusion. Dose banded per national dose bands. Consider draining pleural or peritoneal effusions prior to pemetrexed administration.

Administered in 500ml glucose 5% over 60 minutes, starting 30 minutes after end of pemetrexed infusion. Dose banded at 10mg/ml. Calvert formula using calculated GFR. AUC5 may be considered with EDTA clearance. Max dose 890mg in ARIA (internationally 900mg). Dose may need adjusting if renal function changes >10% between cycles. Seek consultant advice for obese patients or CrCl >125ml/min. Carboplatin is an irritant.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils <1.5x10^9/L — counts remain low after delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction. Stop if myelosuppression recurs.
Delay treatment for 7 days. If counts recover, continue at initial dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Platelets <100x10^9/L — counts remain low after delay
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with 20% dose reduction.
Reduce dose by 50%.
No dose reduction necessary.
Clinical decision required.
Do not administer if CrCl <20ml/min. GFR changes >10% may require dose adjustment.
If creatinine clearance falls below 45ml/min, consider dose reduction (clinical decision).

pre_medications: Folic acid 5mg once daily oral starting 1-2 weeks prior to first pemetrexed dose and continuing for 3 weeks after last dose (mandatory)
Hydroxocobalamin 1mg intramuscular injection every 3 months starting 1-2 weeks prior to first pemetrexed dose (mandatory — prescribed separately from ARIA regimen)
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral starting day before chemotherapy (skin rash prophylaxis — mandatory)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy
Dexamethasone 4mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take home, starting day before next cycle chemotherapy is due — given as supply for next cycle)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take home)
On occasions where patient forgets pre-dose dexamethasone: administer dexamethasone 20mg IV bolus on day of treatment
special_warnings: First line therapy of advanced pleural mesothelioma where disease is not suitable for surgical resection. WHO Performance Status 0, 1. Palliative intent.
Folic acid supplementation is MANDATORY — must begin 1-2 weeks before and continue 3 weeks after last pemetrexed dose.
Hydroxocobalamin (vitamin B12) supplementation is MANDATORY — 1mg IM every 3 months starting 1-2 weeks before first pemetrexed dose.
Dexamethasone skin rash prophylaxis is MANDATORY — must be taken starting the day before pemetrexed.
Carboplatin must be administered 30 minutes after end of pemetrexed infusion.
Consider draining pleural or peritoneal effusions prior to pemetrexed administration.
Pemetrexed is an inflammitant; carboplatin is an irritant.
Number of cycles: 3-6 cycles (6 cycles set in ARIA). Clinical decision on 3 vs 6 cycles.
Consider blood transfusion if patient symptomatic of anaemia or haemoglobin <8g/dL.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required.

drugs.notes: Continuous dosing, 450mg once daily with food. Available as 150mg capsules (3 capsules). Swallow whole — do not chew or crush. If dose missed, patient should make up dose unless next dose due within 12 hours. If vomiting occurs, do not take additional dose — continue with next scheduled dose. If dose reduction required for ADR not listed, reduce by decrements of 150mg daily. Discontinue if patient cannot tolerate 150mg once daily with food. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert applies.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity NCI-CTC grade 1 or 2 (except lymphopenia)
dose_modifications.notes: Continue at same dose; monitor as clinically indicated.
Interrupt until toxicity reduces to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below, then restart at same dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity NCI-CTC grade 4 — first or second occurrence (except lymphopenia)
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until toxicity reduces to NCI-CTC grade 2 or below, then restart with dose reduced by 150mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Haematological toxicity NCI-CTC grade 4 — further recurrence (except lymphopenia)
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue permanently.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic toxicity AST or ALT >5xULN and bilirubin <2xULN
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt until reduced to NCI-CTC grade 1 or baseline, then restart with dose reduced by 150mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic toxicity AST or ALT >3xULN and bilirubin >2xULN (in absence of cholestasis or haemolysis)
dose_modifications.notes: Discontinue permanently.
No dose adjustment required in mild hepatic impairment.

dose_modifications.condition: Pre-existing hepatic impairment — moderate to severe
dose_modifications.notes: Not recommended in moderate to severe hepatic impairment.
No starting dose adjustment required.

dose_modifications.condition: Severe or intolerable nausea, vomiting or diarrhoea despite optimal anti-emetic/anti-diarrhoeal therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until improved, then reinitiate with dose reduced by 150mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Persistent hyperglycaemia >250mg/dl despite optimal anti-hyperglycaemic therapy
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until hyperglycaemia adequately controlled, then reinitiate with dose reduced by 150mg. If adequate glucose control cannot be achieved, permanently discontinue.
Withhold until returns to NCI-CTC grade 1, then reinitiate with dose reduced by 150mg.
Withhold if pneumonitis suspected; permanently discontinue if treatment-related pneumonitis confirmed. All pneumonitis cases in trials occurred within 2 months of initiation.

dose_modifications.condition: QTc >500msec on at least two separate ECGs
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until recovery to baseline or QTc ≤480msec; check and correct electrolytes; reinitiate with dose reduced by 150mg.

dose_modifications.condition: QTc >500msec or >60msec change from baseline with torsade de pointes or polymorphic VT or signs/symptoms of serious arrhythmia
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.

Withhold until recovery to asymptomatic bradycardia or HR ≥60bpm. Evaluate concomitant medications. If contributing drug identified and stopped/adjusted, resume at previous dose. If no contributing drug or not adjusted, reduce by 150mg.

dose_modifications.condition: Life-threatening bradycardia — no contributing concomitant drug identified
dose_modifications.notes: Permanently discontinue.

pre_medications: Loperamide 4mg oral stat after first loose stool then 2-4mg when required for diarrhoea (maximum 16mg/24 hours)
Metoclopramide 10mg oral three times a day when required for nausea and vomiting
special_warnings: ALK-positive NSCLC confirmed required — untreated or post-crizotinib second/subsequent line (per NICE TA500 and TA395).
QTc prolongation monitoring required — ECG monitoring as clinically indicated. Risk increased with concomitant antiarrhythmics, pre-existing cardiac disease, bradycardia, or electrolyte disturbances.
Hepatotoxicity — monitor LFTs every 2 weeks for first month then monthly (SPC recommends every 2 weeks for first 3 months). Ceritinib is largely eliminated via the liver.
Severe, life-threatening, or fatal treatment-related pneumonitis reported — all cases within 2 months of initiation. Monitor for pulmonary symptoms.
Hyperglycaemia reported — monitor blood glucose.
Pancreatitis — monitor lipase/amylase.
Ceritinib interacts with numerous medications — always check for drug interactions.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert must be followed.
Continuous 28-day cycle dosing — take with food, swallow whole.
FBC and U&Es every 4 weeks.
CT scan within 1 month before starting and repeat within 3 months of starting (or earlier if necessary).
Chest x-ray before starting and every 4 weeks as appropriate.
Discontinue if patient cannot tolerate 150mg once daily.

drugs.notes: Administered in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride at maximum rate of 1mg cisplatin/minute (minimum infusion time 60 minutes). Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Cisplatin is an exfoliant. Full hydration protocol required (see pre-medications). Patients must drink at least 2 litres of fluid in 24 hours after cisplatin administration.

Administered in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. Etoposide administered after cisplatin — the 1000ml NaCl 0.9% containing etoposide forms the post-hydration for cisplatin (no additional post-hydration fluid required). Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Etoposide is an irritant.

dose_modifications.notes: Delay both cisplatin and etoposide until counts recover to eligible levels.

dose_modifications.condition: Febrile neutropenia or grade 4 neutropenia >7 days, or grade 4 thrombocytopenia requiring medical intervention, or grade 2+ bleeding with thrombocytopenia — first episode
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 80% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Second episode following first dose reduction
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 50% of original dose.
Stop treatment.
No adjustment necessary.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment Bilirubin 26-51 µmol/L or AST 60-180 units — Etoposide
dose_modifications.notes: 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST >180 units — Etoposide
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision required.
100% dose.
75% of original dose.
Do not use.
100% dose.
75% of original dose.
50% of original dose.
Delay treatment until adverse effect resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then reduce both agents to 75% of original dose.

Delay until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then restart cisplatin at 50% (etoposide remains at previous level). Alternatively substitute cisplatin with carboplatin AUC6 (calculated CrCl) or AUC5 (EDTA clearance).

pre_medications: Dexamethasone 8mg oral or IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (each day)
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy (each day)
Furosemide 40mg when required oral or IV bolus (pre-hydration)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 500ml with 8mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 30 minutes (pre-hydration)
Post-hydration: Etoposide administered in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% serves as post-hydration — no additional post-hydration fluid required
Patients must drink at least 2 litres of fluid in 24 hours after cisplatin
Dexamethasone 4mg once a day oral for 2 days (take home, starting day after chemotherapy finishes)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg on evening of each day of chemotherapy and then 8mg twice a day for 2 days starting day after chemotherapy finishes (take home)
special_warnings: Limited stage SCLC only — usually given concurrently with radical thoracic radiotherapy. Radical intent. WHO Performance Status 0, 1, 2.
Full cisplatin hydration protocol MANDATORY — pre-hydration with 500ml NaCl 0.9% with 8mmol MgSO4 over 30 minutes. Patient must drink at least 2 litres fluid in 24 hours post-cisplatin.
Cisplatin is nephrotoxic — EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before first cycle. Monitor renal function carefully.
Cisplatin is ototoxic — consider formal audiology test if relevant.
Cisplatin peripheral neuropathy — common complication. Reduce to 50% or substitute with carboplatin if grade 2+ neuropathy.
Cisplatin is an exfoliant; etoposide is an irritant — appropriate extravasation precautions required.
Haemoglobin should be kept above 12g/dL if radiotherapy is being given concurrently.
Etoposide administered in 1000ml NaCl forms the post-hydration — no additional fluid required after etoposide.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Growth factors as per local policy.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required.

drugs.notes: Administered in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% with 20mmol potassium chloride at maximum rate of 1mg cisplatin/minute (minimum infusion time 120 minutes). Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Cisplatin is an exfoliant. Full hydration protocol required. Patients must drink at least 3 litres of fluid in 24 hours after cisplatin administration.
Day 1 only: administered in 1000ml sodium chloride 0.9% over 60 minutes. This forms the post-hydration for cisplatin. Dose banded per CSCCN agreed bands. Etoposide is an irritant.

Days 2 and 3 only: oral etoposide once daily. Dose rounded to nearest 50mg (up if halfway). Etoposide should be taken 1 hour before food or on an empty stomach. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert MUST be followed. Short course — must NOT be continued beyond days 2 and 3. Must not be supplied from primary care. Patients must be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting. [VERIFIED: Oral etoposide 240mg/m² over days 2-4 in cisplatin-etoposide SCLC is an established regimen — verify against source PDF]

dose_modifications.notes: Delay both cisplatin and etoposide until counts recover to eligible levels.

dose_modifications.condition: Febrile neutropenia or grade 4 neutropenia >7 days, or grade 4 thrombocytopenia requiring medical intervention, or grade 2+ bleeding with thrombocytopenia — first episode
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 80% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Second episode following first dose reduction
dose_modifications.notes: Reduce dose to 50% of original dose.
Stop treatment.
No adjustment necessary.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment Bilirubin 26-51 µmol/L or AST 60-180 units — Etoposide
dose_modifications.notes: 50% of original dose.

dose_modifications.condition: Hepatic impairment Bilirubin >51 µmol/L or AST >180 units — Etoposide
dose_modifications.notes: Clinical decision required.
100% dose.
75% of original dose.
Do not use.
100% dose.
75% of original dose.
50% of original dose.
Delay treatment until adverse effect resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then reduce both agents to 75% of original dose.

Delay until resolves to NCI-CTC grade 1 or below, then restart cisplatin at 50% (etoposide remains at previous level). Alternatively substitute cisplatin with carboplatin AUC6 (calculated CrCl) or AUC5 (EDTA clearance).

pre_medications: Aprepitant 125mg oral 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 only
Dexamethasone 4mg oral or IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 only
Ondansetron 8mg oral or IV bolus 15-30 minutes prior to chemotherapy on day 1 only
Furosemide 40mg oral or IV bolus (pre-hydration, day 1)
Sodium chloride 0.9% 1000ml with 20mmol potassium chloride and 16mmol magnesium sulphate IV over 60 minutes (pre-hydration, day 1)
Post-hydration: Etoposide 120mg/m² IV in 1000ml NaCl 0.9% over 60 minutes serves as post-hydration on day 1 — no additional post-hydration fluid required
Patients must drink at least 3 litres of fluid in 24 hours after cisplatin
Etoposide 240mg/m² oral once daily on days 2 and 3 (take home — oral SACT, short course only, NPSA alert applies)
Aprepitant 80mg once a day oral for 2 days (take home)
Dexamethasone 4mg once a day oral for 3 days (take home)
Metoclopramide 10mg three times a day when required oral (take home)
Ondansetron 8mg twice a day oral for 3 days (take home)
special_warnings: Limited stage SCLC only — usually given concurrently with radical thoracic radiotherapy. Radical intent. WHO Performance Status 0, 1, 2.
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert MUST be followed for oral etoposide (days 2 and 3).
Oral etoposide is a SHORT COURSE (days 2 and 3 only) — must NOT be continued beyond this. All staff including community staff must be clearly informed.
Patients must be assessed for suitability for oral chemotherapy prior to starting treatment.
Oral etoposide must not be supplied from primary care.

Full cisplatin hydration protocol MANDATORY — pre-hydration with 1000ml NaCl 0.9% with 20mmol KCl and 16mmol MgSO4 over 60 minutes. Patient must drink at least 3 litres fluid in 24 hours post-cisplatin.
Cisplatin is nephrotoxic — EDTA or calculated creatinine clearance required before first cycle. Monitor renal function carefully.
Cisplatin is ototoxic — consider formal audiology test if relevant.
Cisplatin peripheral neuropathy — common complication. Reduce to 50% or substitute with carboplatin if grade 2+ neuropathy.
Cisplatin is an exfoliant; etoposide (IV) is an irritant — appropriate extravasation precautions required.
Haemoglobin should be kept above 12g/dL if radiotherapy is being given concurrently.
IV etoposide on day 1 forms the post-hydration for cisplatin — no additional fluid required.
If a third dose reduction is necessary, treatment should be stopped.
Growth factors as per local policy.
Gastric protection with PPI or H2 antagonist may be considered in patients at high risk of GI ulceration or bleed.
Prophylactic antibiotics can be considered if required.
